rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes, not implementation scopes, unless binding to subcomponent ports.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax for all AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: Align helper declaration types with actual expression types (bool, int, real) to avoid type mismatch diagnostics.
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers; use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens (ms, Hz) as AGREE variables.
Do not generate unsafe clauses for Boolean data ports without explicit scalar representation in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing constraints not directly tied to typed ports or constants
Implementation-level bindings and subcomponent port usage in component types
Model Analyst Agent's role in extracting AADL structure
